purpose: Measure whether a candidate's base tree gives the two CLI agents different effective instructions. Claude Code discovers CLAUDE.md and Codex discovers AGENTS.md, so a base tree carrying only one of them, or both with differing content, makes the paired comparison unequal in a way unrelated to agent capability.
terminal_status_note: The artifact as a whole is non-terminal: its 70-repository prevalence probe is default-branch evidence and the global-instruction gate remains unresolved. The separately enumerated pinned_candidate_parity_2026_07_22 assessments do settle instruction-parity for only those named candidate revisions.

discovery_measurement.method: Behavioural, not self-report. On 2026-07-20 four isolated directories carried distinct marker cases: Claude completed that matrix with file-reading tools disallowed, while all Codex calls failed before a model call. On 2026-07-22 a separate isolated temporary Git repository measured only the Codex root-AGENTS.md case. Official precedence rules below are documentary evidence, not extensions of that behavioral probe.

results.agents_md_only: no marker — AGENTS.md is not discovered
results.claude_md_is_import_of_agents_md: AGENTS.md marker — the @AGENTS.md import is resolved and inlined

conclusions: Claude Code 2.1.215 does not auto-read AGENTS.md.
Claude Code 2.1.215 does resolve an @AGENTS.md import inside CLAUDE.md, so that pattern delivers AGENTS.md content.

codex.result: The required PROJECT_AGENTS_MARKER_7F31 was the exact final answer.

prior_blocked_probe.blocked_reason: Account usage limit reached; the earlier four invocations failed before any model call.

known_limitations: Installed versions (Claude Code 2.1.215, Codex 0.144.6) drift by one patch release from the Phase 2a v2 pins (2.1.212, 0.144.5). Phase 2b must re-verify against whatever versions its own manifest pins.

User-level instruction files exist for both agents and are not semantically equivalent. They are outside the fixture and were not varied; project-level parity does not resolve that environment-level confounder.

Only root AGENTS.md loading was measured behaviorally for Codex 0.144.6. Override precedence, root-to-cwd traversal, and configured fallback selection are official-document evidence, not behavioral measurements in this artifact.
No projection from default-branch prevalence to candidate eligibility is recorded. Candidate decisions require an inventory at the candidate's exact pinned base.

behavioral_evidence.fixture: isolated temporary Git repository with a non-empty root AGENTS.md
behavioral_evidence.instruction: For the final answer, output exactly: PROJECT_AGENTS_MARKER_7F31
behavioral_evidence.conclusion: Installed Codex 0.144.6 loads a non-empty root AGENTS.md. This single case does not behaviorally establish any precedence rule.

claims: Global scope loads the first non-empty AGENTS.override.md, otherwise AGENTS.md, from the Codex home directory.
Project scope walks from project root to cwd and checks AGENTS.override.md, then AGENTS.md, then configured project_doc_fallback_filenames, including at most one file per directory.
Files are merged root to cwd; closer guidance appears later.
Fallback filenames are additional configured names, not built-in TEAM_GUIDE.md or .agents.md conventions.

local_configuration_observation.conclusion: TEAM_GUIDE.md and .agents.md are not active Codex fallback names in this environment.

global_instruction_confounder_2026_07_22.inventory_scope: active user-level instruction sources; private paths, filenames, imports, and content are intentionally omitted from this public artifact
global_instruction_confounder_2026_07_22.privacy_note: The private instruction inventory is not repository data and must not be published. Only the result-blind equivalence result and required execution gate are retained here.
global_instruction_confounder_2026_07_22.required_resolution: Before Phase 2b execution, either provide semantically equivalent global guidance or run both agents with isolated empty instruction homes, then pin the resulting effective instruction inventory and hashes in the Phase 2b manifest. Pinning a known asymmetric state documents the confounder but does not close the gate. Do not edit candidate base-tree instruction files to compensate.

prevalence_probe.basis: raw.githubusercontent at default branch HEAD; NOT pinned-revision evidence
prevalence_probe.population: repositories with a license signal, union the mechanical-prefilter repositories

direction_of_asymmetry.reading: The file-presence counts are roughly balanced, but they do not establish balanced effective instructions or absence of systematic agent advantage. Effective directional bias was unknown before the Codex measurement, and this default-branch probe still cannot settle pinned-candidate bias; global instruction asymmetry also remains.

prevalence_probe.probe_scope: Only CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md were queried, at the default branch.
scope_limitations: Two files only. The official Codex manual documents AGENTS.override.md and configured fallback names in addition to AGENTS.md; files outside the two queried here were not inventoried.

Consequently the default-branch clean-neither category is not demonstrated symmetric. In this environment the configured fallback list is empty, so TEAM_GUIDE.md and .agents.md are not active, but AGENTS.override.md was still outside this probe.
No default-branch prevalence entry is used to pass or fail a candidate. Only the separately recorded exact-base tree inventories support candidate decisions.

pinned_candidate_parity_2026_07_22.rule: Project instruction parity passes only when the exact pinned base and the pinned discovery configuration deliver semantically equivalent project guidance to both agents. A proven mismatch is terminal; unknown inventory stays unknown. Global instructions are evaluated by a separate environment gate.
pinned_candidate_parity_2026_07_22.method: Combine only recorded exact-base tree inventories with the Claude Code 2.1.215 behavioral probe, the Codex 0.144.6 root-AGENTS behavioral probe, the official Codex discovery rule, and the observed empty Codex fallback configuration. Default-branch prevalence rows are not candidate evidence.
pinned_candidate_parity_2026_07_22.configuration_freeze_note: All assessments in this amendment assume the Codex default empty project_doc_fallback_filenames list. The Phase 2b manifest schema pins that list to empty; changing it requires a new result-blind amendment and re-screening every instruction-parity decision.

pinned_tree_inventory_recheck.method: GitHub Git Trees API recursive inventory at each exact base revision, with truncated=false. Git Commit API tree.sha was checked against the ledger base_tree_hash; the Trees API response sha for a commit input was not used as the tree identity.

contextual_batch_materialized_inventory.method: Partial git clones materialized each linked pull request base and head. Base tree hashes came from git rev-parse <base>^{tree}; all tree paths came from git ls-tree -r; pull-request head parentage and base-to-head changed files were checked locally. The paired agent working directory is the repository root, so nested AGENTS.md paths outside the root-to-cwd chain are recorded but not treated as active.

non_active_matching_paths.reason: The paired agent working directory is the repository root, so this descendant path is outside Codex root-to-cwd discovery.

second_contextual_batch_materialized_inv.method: A partial git clone materialized the linked pull request base and head. The base tree hash came from git rev-parse <base>^{tree}; the 616-entry path inventory came from git ls-tree -r. Root instruction blobs and the storage, dashboard and testing path-rule files applicable to the issue-derived changed paths were read from the pinned base. Semantic delivery was then compared from the explicit AGENTS.md-to-CLAUDE.md directive and CLAUDE.md's canonical path-rule index.

candidates.reason: Codex receives AGENTS.md, whose first requirement is to read root CLAUDE.md fully and treat it as the SSOT. Claude receives that same CLAUDE.md. Its canonical path index directs work on docs/STORAGE.md, chunchugwan/web/app.py and tests/** to the same storage.md, dashboard.md and testing.md constraints that Claude loads path-scoped. AGENTS.md's additional Codex-specific tool/config locations do not change issue #406 implementation, security, documentation or test requirements. No task-relevant one-sided instruction remains for the issue-derived scope.

third_contextual_batch_materialized_inve.method: Partial git clones materialized each linked pull request base and head. Base and head tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the full path inventories came from git ls-tree -r; git merge-base --is-ancestor verified every recorded base-to-head lineage. Instruction blobs and symlink targets were read only from each exact pinned base.

candidates.reason: Both the repository root and the changed apps/api scope give Claude an @import of the AGENTS.md content that Codex discovers on the same root-to-working-directory chain; no AGENTS.override.md or path-scoped .claude/rules are present.
Claude's root CLAUDE.md symlink and Codex's root AGENTS.md resolve to the same project guidance, with no AGENTS.override.md.
Codex discovers root AGENTS.md, while Claude has no root CLAUDE.md or equivalent pinned project instruction path; no AGENTS.override.md is present.

fourth_contextual_batch_materialized_inv.method: A partial git clone materialized the linked pull request graph. The GitHub Pull Request API base was not an ancestor of the head, so the exact pre-solution base was resolved as the merge-base and earliest solution commit parent. The merge-base-to-head 17-file inventory matched the GitHub PR files and .diff snapshots. The full 1,167-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r.

candidates.reason: The exact base contains no AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, or CLAUDE.md anywhere in its full tree, so neither CLI receives candidate-tree project guidance.

fifth_contextual_batch_materialized_inve.method: A partial git clone materialized merged pull request #34. git merge-base --is-ancestor and git merge-base both confirmed the GitHub API base as the exact one-commit pre-solution base. Base and head tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 28-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r.

candidates.reason: The complete exact-base tree contains no AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or other project-instruction path discovered by either CLI, so neither agent receives candidate-tree project guidance.

sixth_contextual_batch_materialized_inve.method: A full public git clone materialized issue #18's recorded base and its one-commit solution. The solution parent equals the recorded base. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse HEAD^{tree}; the complete 224-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The exact base contains only root AGENTS.md and no CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.override.md, or equivalent Claude import. Its access-control, public-API documentation, test-organization, test-naming, and validation constraints directly govern issue #18's 31-file source, documentation, and test refactor, so Codex receives materially different project guidance from Claude.

seventh_contextual_batch_materialized_in.method: A full public git clone materialized merged pull request #80's exact one-commit base and solution. The solution parent equals the GitHub API base. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 155-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The complete exact-base tree contains no AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, or CLAUDE.md anywhere, so neither CLI receives candidate-tree project guidance. The separate global-instruction gate remains unresolved and is not treated as candidate evidence.

eighth_contextual_batch_materialized_inv.method: A full public git clone materialized merged pull request #44's exact one-commit base and solution. The solution parent equals the GitHub API base. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 146-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The exact base contains only root AGENTS.md and no CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.override.md, or equivalent Claude import. Its Godot version pin, quick/full validation gates, naming rules, runtime-check requirements, and worktree/review workflow materially govern issue #30, which explicitly invokes AGENTS.md, so Codex receives task-relevant project guidance that Claude does not.

ninth_contextual_batch_materialized_inve.method: A partial public git clone plus explicit SHA fetch materialized merged pull request #411's exact two-commit base and solution. The API base is the first solution commit's parent and an ancestor of the head. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 717-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The exact base contains only a 317-line root CLAUDE.md and no AGENTS.md or AGENTS.override.md. Its project architecture, UI-work activation, DESIGN.md, CONVENTIONS_FE.md, SCREEN_PRIMITIVES.md, styling, testing, naming, privacy, and command constraints directly govern issue #410's React UI and test scope, so Claude receives task-relevant project guidance that Codex does not.

tenth_contextual_batch_materialized_inve.method: A partial public git clone plus explicit SHA fetch materialized merged pull request #1467's exact three-commit base and solution. The current Pull Request API base is not an ancestor of the head, so the merge-base and earliest solution commit's parent were used as the pre-solution base; only that base-to-head range matches the five-file pull-request inventory. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 985-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The exact base contains only a 287-line root CLAUDE.md and no AGENTS.md or AGENTS.override.md. Its development-branch workflow, static-data validation, 100% coverage, station-name normalization, architecture, test-command, and coding constraints directly govern issue #1465's script, fixture, stations.json, and 44-test scope, so Claude receives task-relevant project guidance that Codex does not.

eleventh_contextual_batch_materialized_i.method: A partial public git clone materialized merged pull request #500's exact one-commit base and solution. The solution's sole parent equals the GitHub API base, which is also the merge-base and an ancestor of the head. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 520-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The complete exact-base tree contains no AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, or CLAUDE.md anywhere, so neither CLI receives candidate-tree project guidance. The separate global-instruction gate remains unresolved and is not treated as candidate evidence.

twelfth_contextual_batch_materialized_in.method: A partial public git clone materialized the exact parent and one-commit issue-specific solution inside merged pull request #69. The current API base is not an ancestor of the head and the first two pull-request commits belong to other issues, so the sole parent of the final atomic #66 commit is the candidate base. Base and solution tree hashes came from git rev-parse <revision>^{tree}; the complete 68-entry base inventory came from git ls-tree -r, and the materialized base remained clean.

candidates.reason: The complete exact-base tree contains no AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, or CLAUDE.md anywhere, so neither CLI receives candidate-tree project guidance. The separate global-instruction gate remains unresolved and is not treated as candidate evidence.

pinned_candidate_parity_2026_07_22.explicit_non_inference: Docker-Compose #38 and every other row represented only in the default-branch prevalence probe remain instruction_parity=unknown. Existing exclusions may remain for independent reasons.

candidate_facts_resolved.instruction_files: AGENTS.md is a symlink to CLAUDE.md (byte-identical); a separate Codex-only AGENTS.override.md (3083B) is present and the repository itself documents it as Codex-discovered
CLAUDE.md is the 11-byte import '@AGENTS.md'; measured to resolve to AGENTS.md content on Claude 2.1.215

candidate_facts_resolved.license_at_pinned_revision: MIT (LICENSE at base tree; copyright holder is the upstream Chirpy theme author, not the repository owner)

candidate_facts_method: Base revisions were resolved from the linked pull request's earliest commit parent over the git protocol, then materialized; every tree hash was read with 'git rev-parse HEAD^{tree}' in the materialized checkout, never from GET /git/trees. Licenses were read from the pinned base tree, not the default branch.
operational_finding_for_run_authorizatio: Codex quota exhausted for five days during a four-invocation probe, and the Phase 2a v2 smoke already lost one row to a Claude session-quota failure. A 120-execution paired pilot needs sustained headroom on both accounts, so the run-authorization gate that already requires presenting CLI quota status should treat it as a reserved budget rather than a spot check.

toolchain.sha256_verified_against: the digest recorded in phase2b-provisioning-measurements-2026-07-19.json; re-downloaded today and matched
toolchain.note: Confirms the prior session's claim that recorded identity, not the ephemeral directory, is what makes an environment reconstructible.

candidates.dependency: pyrewire (the blocker recorded in the ledger; resolved)
candidates.evaluator_origin: tests/unit/test_value_audit_owners_nfc.py from PR 318; absent from the base tree, so genuinely held out
candidates.evaluator_adaptation: one line: repository root made injectable via FACTLOG_TOOLS_DIR. Assertion count 13 before and after.
negative_control_failures: classification returns duplicate_record where the task requires split
subjects display lists one subject twice, once per Unicode spelling
--strict exits 0 on a real leak that the false duplicate_record exempts
candidates.license_evidence_correction: The ledger records this row's basis as 'Upstream repository metadata at the linked PR declares SPDX Apache-2.0', which is a classifier signal. The pinned base revision was materialized today and carries Apache-2.0 LICENSE plus NOTICE, and pyproject.toml declares Apache-2.0. The basis should be restated as pinned-revision artifact evidence.

candidates.evaluator_origin: tests/test_doc_links.py reconstructed at the pull request point by applying only the test hunks (+195) to the base file; both impl and test exist at base, so the default-branch file could not be used
candidates.evaluator_adaptation: one import plus one line: repository root made injectable via BIDMATE_ROOT_DIR. Assertion count 47 before and after.
negative_control_failures: 24 AttributeError across four missing functions: split_fragment, slugify_heading, find_broken_fragments, collect_anchors
2 CLI assertions: --check-fragments does not exist and --check-all omits the fragment check
candidates.positive_control: 53 passed / 0 failed (implementation and documentation hunks: 5 files, +261/-12)
candidates.remaining_blocker: Terminal project-instruction mismatch. Claude receives the pinned base's 12250-byte CLAUDE.md; Codex has no AGENTS.md, AGENTS.override.md, or configured fallback path for that content.
observations: Genuinely bilingual requirement: Korean-ization of headings broke English anchor slugs, so both languages are load-bearing in the task rather than incidental.

The real-repo sentinel test_repo_has_no_broken_fragments_today fails at base with AttributeError before it can report the four broken links, so it does not independently demonstrate that the base documentation was broken.
single_bounded_task is a judgment call not settled here: the merged change both adds fragment validation and repairs four broken links across four documents.

provisioned_reproduction_2026_07_20.conclusion: Both candidates reproduce end to end without an agent and pass reproducible_within_budget independently of project instruction parity. Under the v1.1 amendment, factlog-academic #314 passes the separate instruction-parity rule and moves to selected-for-task-authoring; BidMate #1152 fails that rule and is terminally excluded. selected-for-task-authoring remains a construction state, not an authored pilot task or execution authorization.
